info.title: AcquiredData API
info.description: The Acquired Data Autonomous Service (AcquiredDataAS) allows CRUD and processing operations on injection data.

get.summary: Gets the channel(s) information for the specified injection.

parameters.name: channelFilter
parameters.description:
The channel filter allow specifying the channel(s) on which spectra processing should be applied. You can filter by channelId or energyLevel based on the examples provided.
Please note:
  • energyLevel filter is only compatible with MSe mode.
    • Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | channelId | The channel Id| | energyLevel| The energy level that can be low or high| Notes:
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported logical operator is: and
  • Nested conditions via grouping operators like () are not supported.
  • Mixed filter combining different fields is not supported.

200.description:
Returns the properties of the channels in an injection.
The Channels contain various data (spectra, chromatograms, etc.), differentiated by the 'ChannelDataShape' property.
Data discovery on the channels can be performed via `scanning method`, as follows:
-DDA data discovery is made via `isDDA` flag set to true; additionally DDA data discovery is executed via scanning method:
  • MS for the DDA MS data acquired with waters_connect,
  • MSMS for the DDA MSMS data acquired with waters_connect or MassLynx,
  • PRODUCT for MSMS data acquired with MassLynx

    • -Non-DDA data discovery is made via scanning method:
  • MS for MS data,
  • MSMS for MSMS data,
  • MS for MSe data; additionally the `energy level` set to Low or High, as opposed to Not set for MS data,
  • MRM, SIR, PRECURSOR, PRODUCT, and NeutralLoss for their corresponding data: MRM, SIR, PRECURSOR, PRODUCT, and NeutralLoss,
  • PRODUCT for MSMS data acquired with MassLynx
    • The `scanning method` and `energy level` information is part of analytical technique.

get.summary: Gets the MS spectra for the specified injection and retention time or scan index.

parameters.name: channelFilter
parameters.description:
The channel filter allow specifying the channel(s) on which spectra processing should be applied. You can filter by channelId or energyLevel based on the examples provided.
Please note:
  • energyLevel filter is only compatible with MSe mode.
    • Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | channelId | The channel Id| | energyLevel| The energy level that can be low or high| Notes:
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported logical operator is: and
  • Nested conditions via grouping operators like () are not supported.
  • Mixed filter combining different fields is not supported.
  • EnergyLevel filter is only compatible with MSe mode.

parameters.name: scanFilter
parameters.description:
Filters the response data based on retention times or scan indexes of one or more scans within the data set.
Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | rt | retention time (in minutes) of a scan or multiple scans | | index| scan index (position in the scan sequence) of a scan or multiple scans | Notes:
  • Supported logical operator is: and.
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported range operators are: gt (greater than), lt (less than), ge (greater than or equal), le(less than or equal).
  • Two types of comparisons are possible: equality comparison(eq) and range comparison(gt, lt, ge, le).
  • The range operators can be used individually as well as combinations of them like: (ge, le), (gt, lt), (ge, lt), (gt, le) with the use of (and) logical operator.
  • Multiple ranges are not supported.
  • The (eq) operator returns either the exact match, if found, or the nearest value to the received input.
  • Two types of comparisons are possible in a filter expression: equality comparison (eq) or range comparison (gt, lt, ge, le).
  • Nested conditions via grouping operators like () are not supported.
  • Mixed filter combining different fields is not supported.

parameters.name: massFilter
parameters.description:
The mass filter, allows to select the data associated with the specified mass filter.
Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | setMassMz | The set mass m/z is applicable to data obtained through the following scanning methods: NEUTRAL LOSS, MSMS, PRODUCT. | | precursorMz (with window)| The precursor m/z is applicable to data obtained through the following scanning methods: PRODUCT, DIA_HE_STEPPED_QUAD. Window is optional and is only supported for Sonar data.| | productMz| The product m/z is applicable to data obtained through the following scanning methods: PRECURSOR.| Notes:
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Mixed filter combining different fields is not supported.
  • Multiple conditions on the same field are not supported.
  • On waters_connect DDA data, which utilizes scanning method MSMS the setMassMz filter is applicable.
  • On NeutralLoss Data the setMassMz filter is applicable at channel level, while on MSMS/Product, it will filter at scan level.
  • On MASSLYNX DDA data, which utilizes scanning method PRODUCT the precursorMz filter is applicable, along with the setMass m/z filter.
  • The filter values can be retrieved from the BasicMSProperties object within the Technique response obtained by querying the endpoint GET /InjectionData/{injectionId}/channels.
  • Filter precision is set at five decimal places.
  • The precursorMz is applicable at scan level for Sonar data, otherwise is applicable at channel level.
  • The filter values can be retrieved from the BasicMSProperties and QuadProperties objects within the Technique response obtained by querying the endpoint GET /injection-data/{injectionId}/channels.

Retrieves data associated with multiple.value: precursorMz eq 109.40033 with window eq 0.5 and precursorMz eq 119.40033 with window eq 0.2

parameters.name: spectrumType
parameters.description:
Specify the type of spectrum data to be returned for scanning channels.
</br>
Supported filter fields:
|Field|Meaning|
|---|---|
|Continuum |Returns a continuum spectrum providing masses and intensities for continuous signals.|
|Centered	|Returns a centered spectrum with centered data, consisted of the centroid of the peak (the data representing the peak's center of gravity). |
|PreciselyCentered|Returns a centered spectrum with precisely centered data, obtained through an advanced algorithm (Mass Measure operation). This functionality is applicable only to data sets acquired using TOF instruments.|
|CenteredAndContinuum | Returns a spectrum consisting of both centered and continuum data|
|PreciselyCenteredAndContinuum	 |Returns a spectrum consisting of both precisely centered and continuum data. This functionality is applicable only to data sets acquired using TOF instruments.|
Note:
</br>
   The default spectrum type is Continuum

parameters.name: expand
parameters.description:
Comma separated list of any number of expand options. Enables retrieval of sub-resources and additional information. If the requested additional data via the Expand option is not available, it will be omitted from the response without causing an error. The supported expand options are:
  • technique

Please note:

  • the GET/ms/combined-spectra endpoint does not combine the data from distinct channels. Instead, it performs a combine operation independently for each channel when multiple channel IDs are specified.
    • Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | channelId | The channel Id| | energyLevel| The energy level that can be low or high| Notes:
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported logical operator is: and
  • Nested conditions via grouping operators like () are not supported.
  • Mixed filter combining different fields is not supported.
  • EnergyLevel filter is only compatible with MSe mode.
  • The GET/ms/combined-spectra endpoint does not combine the data from distinct channels. Instead, it performs a combine operation independently for each channel when multiple channel IDs are specified.

parameters.description:
Filters the response data based on retention times or scan indexes of one or more scans within the data set.
Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | rt | retention time (in minutes) of a scan or multiple scans | | index | scan index (position in the scan sequence) of a scan or multiple scans | Notes:
  • Supported logical operator is: and.
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported range operators are: gt (greater than), lt (less than), ge (greater than or equal), le(less than or equal).
  • Two types of comparisons are possible: equality comparison(eq) and range comparison(gt, lt, ge, le).
  • The range operators can be used individually as well as combinations of them like: (ge, le), (gt, lt), (ge, lt), (gt, le) with the use of (and) logical operator.

parameters.description:

Window is optional and is only supported for Sonar data.| | productMz| The product m/z is applicable to data obtained through the following scanning methods: PRECURSOR.| Notes:

  • Multiple conditions on the same field are not supported.
  • On waters_connect DDA data, which utilizes scanning method MSMS the setMassMz filter is applicable.
  • On NeutralLoss Data the setMassMz filter is applicable at channel level, while on MSMS/Product, it will filter at scan level.
  • On MASSLYNX DDA data, which utilizes scanning method PRODUCT the precursorMz filter is applicable, along with the setMass m/z filter.
  • Filter precision is set at five decimal places.
  • In case of combined-spectra the response data is not grouped by one spectrum per set mass.
  • To precisely determine the scan index to which the set mass m/z or precursor m/z belongs, use GET/ {injectionId}/ms/spectra endpoint.

  • The filter values can be retrieved from the BasicMSProperties and QuadProperties objects within the Technique response obtained by querying the endpoint GET /injection-data/{injectionId}/channels.
    • Note:
    Using the setMassMz filter when requesting PreciselyCentered Data, for MSMS/Product ScanningMethods, is not supported.

Retrieves data associated with multiple.value: precursorMz eq 109.40033 with window eq 0.5 and precursorMz eq 119.40033 with window eq 0.2

get.summary: Generates the DDA Switch Table for the specified injection
get.description:
DDA (Data-Dependent Acquisition) is an acquisition method performing a precursor scan to determine the mass-to-charge ratio (m/z) and abundance of ions currently entering the mass spectrometer, followed by sequence determining MS/MS scans on ions from a subset of detected peaks, based on their intensity. This endpoint retrieves the DDA Switch Table for the specified injection. Each switch represents the moment when the DDA MS acquisition mode stops and the instrument switches to DDA MSMS acquisition mode, and the ion fragmentation starts. Note:
  • This endpoint is intended to be used only by internal waters_connect apps.

200.description:
Returns the DDA Switch Table.
The switches will be retrieved in ascending order, by switching time.
Notes:
  • In order to know if the specified injection was acquired using the DDA acquisition mode, the "isDDA" flag can be used from following endpoint GET /injection-data/{injectionId}/channels

get.summary: Gets a collection of optical spectra for the specified injection.

parameters.name: channelFilter
parameters.description:
The 'channelFilter' allows specifying the channel(s) of interest for filtering.
Please note that only channelId filtering is supported. Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | channelId | The channel ID | Notes:
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported logical operator is: and
  • Nested conditions via grouping operators like () are not supported.

parameters.name: scanFilter
parameters.description:
Filters the response data based on retention times or scan indexes of one or more scans within the data set.
Supported filter fields: |Field | Meaning | |---|---| | rt | retention time (in minutes) of a scan or multiple scans | | index| scan index (position in the scan sequence) of a scan or multiple scans | Notes:
  • Supported logical operator is: and.
  • Supported equality operator is eq (equal).
  • Supported range operators are: gt (greater than), lt (less than), ge (greater than or equal), le(less than or equal).
  • Two types of comparisons are possible: equality comparison(eq) and range comparison(gt, lt, ge, le).
  • The range operators can be used individually as well as combinations of them like: (ge, le), (gt, lt), (ge, lt), (gt, le) with the use of (and) logical operator.
  • Multiple ranges are not supported.
  • The (eq) operator returns either the exact match, if found, or the nearest value to the received input.
  • Two types of comparisons are possible in a filter expression: equality comparison (eq) or range comparison (gt, lt, ge, le).
  • Nested conditions via grouping operators like () are not supported.
  • Mixed filter combining different fields is not supported.
